danger_signals.visual_form: Character sprite damage states / health bar reduction
danger_signals.meaning: Wrestler taking damage from opponent attacks; risk of knockout
Active threat from competitor wrestler during match

safety_signals.meaning: Safe moment to execute moves or recover health
Opponent not actively attacking; window for player action

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: WWE/WWF branded wrestling ring, crowd participation, announcer presence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Active wrestling ring with both wrestlers engaged, referee present, crowd visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Wrestlers execute moves, block, grapple; referee monitors match rules

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Wrestler selection screen, Howard Finkel announcement sequence (unused interview system)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Howard Finkel announces wrestlers; match initiates after selection

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Defeated wrestler knocked out or pinned; visual match-end confirmation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Winning wrestler stands while defeated wrestler remains down; referee confirms victory

environmental_storytelling.example: WWF logos and wrestling ring setup establish professional wrestling context and league identity
Visible audience in arena communicates scale and legitimacy of match as televised sporting event
Referee presence on canvas communicates rule enforcement and professional match structure
Howard Finkel announcements frame matches as broadcast events with individual wrestler identities

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Responds to input; executes selected moves and grapples
Attacks player; closes distance; executes wrestling moves
Reduced attack frequency; vulnerable to finishing moves
Present on canvas; monitors falls and match legality
Reactive audience presence indicating investment in bout outcome

notes: Source material provides limited visual/mechanical details. Game uses professional wrestling broadcast convention to communicate match state—wrestlers, ring, referee, announcer, and crowd form the primary manifestation system. Unused pre-match interview text suggests developer intended deeper wrestler personality communication before matches, but final implementation streamlines to announcer-driven match initiation. No faction-vs-faction or political state systems documented; world-state is purely match-progression focused.
